Submodules’āü

core.base_classes_algorithms module’āü

class core.base_classes_algorithms.AlgorithmsBASE(*args, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: object

The Base-Class for all solvers.

spectrum_is_being_used’āü

what the name says

Type:

bool

fft(signal, sk, rn, axis=-1)’āü
ifft(signal, sk, rn, axis=-1)’āü
generate_signal_t(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Applies calculate_signal_t to a whole population via jax.vmap

calculate_S_prime_individual(signal_t, measured_trace, mu, measurement_info, descent_info, local_or_global)’āü
calculate_S_prime_population(signal_t, measured_trace, mu, measurement_info, descent_info, local_or_global, axes)’āü
do_checks_before_running(**kwargs)’āü

Called by algorithm.run() performs a few checks and applies settings before running the algorithms.

run(init_vals, no_iterations=1, **kwargs)’āü

This function is invoked by most solvers to perform the iterative reconstruction. Excpetions to this are PtychographicIterativeEngine and COPRA. These invoke run_scan() twice in order to perform a local and global optimization phase.

apply_spectrum(pulse, spectral_amplitude, eta)’āü
do_step_and_apply_spectral_amplitude(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, do_step)’āü

If a spectrum is provided this wraps around the step-method of all solvers and projects the current guess onto the measured spectrum.

use_measured_spectrum(frequency, spectrum, pulse_or_gate='pulse')’āü

Needs to be called if a pulse spectrum is meant to be used in the reconstruction.

In Classical Algorithms. The spectrum will be applied as a projection of the pulse onto an average of itself and the spectrum of the current guess. This process can be tuned via eta.

In General Optimization Algorithms, providing the specteum will cause an optimization of the spectral phase only. Therefore eta has no effect, except for LSF.

Parameters:

  • frequency ŌĆō jnp.array, the frequency axis of spectrum

  • spectrum ŌĆō jnp.array, the spectrum

  • pulse_or_gate (str) ŌĆō whether the spectrum is from the pulse or the gate-pulse.

  • eta (float) ŌĆō has to be bigger than zero and not larger than one, (1-eta) * abs(pulse_f) + eta * spectral_amplitude

Returns:

the class instance

class core.base_classes_algorithms.ClassicAlgorithmsBASE(*args, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: AlgorithmsBASE

The Base-Class for all classical solvers (e.g. Generalized Projection, ŌĆ”). Inherits from AlgorithmsBASE.

local_gamma’āü

step size for local iterations

Type:

float

global_gamma’āü

step size for global iterations

Type:

float

linesearch’āü

enables/disables a linesearch. Can be False, backtracking or zoom.

Type:

bool, str

max_steps_linesearch’āü

maximum number of linesearch steos

Type:

int

c1’āü

constant for the Armijo-condition

Type:

float

c2’āü

constant for the strong Wolfe-condition

Type:

float

delta_gamma’āü

a factor which by which gamma is increased each iteration

Type:

float

local_newton’āü

enables/disables the use of a hessian in local iterations. Can be False, lbfgs or diagonal.

Type:

bool, str

global_newton’āü

enables/disables the use of a hessian in global iterations. Can be False, lbfgs, diagonal or full.

Type:

bool, str

lambda_lm’āü

a Levenberg-Marquardt style damping coefficient.

Type:

float

lbfgs_memory’āü

the number of past iterations to use in LBFGS

Type:

int

linalg_solver’āü

chooses a library/method for inverting the hessian. Can be scipy, lineax or a specific lineax solver.

Type:

str, lineax-solver

conjugate_gradients’āü

enables/diables the use of the Nonlinear Conjugate Gradients method. Can be False, Fletcher-Reeves, Hestenes-Stiefel, Dai-Yuan, Polak-Ribiere or average.

Type:

bool, str

r_local_method’āü

chooses the method on the calculation of S_prime in local iterations. Can be projection or iteration.

Type:

str

r_global_method’āü

chooses the method on the calculation of S_prime in global iterations. Can be projection or iteration.

Type:

str

r_gradient’āü

if r_method=iteration, chooses the type of residual to optimize. Can be amplitude or intensity.

Type:

str

r_newton’āü

enables/diables the use of the diagonal hessian if r_method=iteration

Type:

bool

r_no_iterations’āü

the number of iterations if r_method=iteration

Type:

int

xi’āü

a damping coefficient for adaptive step-sizes, avoids division by zero

Type:

float

local_adaptive_scaling’āü

enables/disables adaptive step sized in local iterations. Can be one of False, pade_10 (linear), pade_20 (nonlinear), pade_11, pade_01 or pade_02

Type:

bool, str

global_adaptive_scaling’āü

enables/disables adaptive step sized in global iterations. Can be one of False, pade_10 (linear), pade_20 (nonlinear), pade_11, pade_01 or pade_02

Type:

bool, str

momentum_is_being_used’āü

what the name says

Type:

bool

set_nonlinear_optimization(local_method=False, global_method=False, damping=0.001, memory=10, solver='lineax')’āü

A helper to set attributes. Sets all attributes which are related to (Quasi)-Newton methods. Will overwrite all attributes and may thus cause some unwanted changes.

Parameters:

  • local_method (bool, str) ŌĆō can be one of False, ŌĆ£diagonalŌĆØ, ŌĆ£fullŌĆØ or ŌĆ£lbfgsŌĆØ

  • global_method (bool, str) ŌĆō can be one of False, ŌĆ£diagonalŌĆØ, ŌĆ£fullŌĆØ or ŌĆ£lbfgsŌĆØ

  • damping (float) ŌĆō a Levenberg-Marquardt style damping factor

  • memory (int) ŌĆō the memory used in LBFGS

  • solver (str, lineax-solver) ŌĆō the solver used if method==ŌĆØfullŌĆØ, can be one of ŌĆ£lineaxŌĆØ, ŌĆ£scipyŌĆØ or an instance of a lineax-solver

Returns:

self

set_linesearch(method=False, max_steps=10, c1=0.0001, c2=0.9, delta_gamma=0.5)’āü

A helper to set attributes. Sets all attributes which are related to linesearches. Will overwrite all attributes and may thus cause some unwanted changes.

Parameters:

  • method (bool, str) ŌĆō the linesearch method, can be one of False, ŌĆ£backtrackingŌĆØ or ŌĆ£zoomŌĆØ

  • max_steps (int) ŌĆō the max number of steps per linesearch

  • c1 (float) ŌĆō the constant of the armijo-condition

  • c2 (float) ŌĆō the constant of the wolfe-condition

  • delta_gamma (float) ŌĆō a factor that scales the stepsize, for backtracking delta_gamma<1, for zoom delta_gamma>1

Returns:

self

set_adaptive_stepsize(local_method=False, global_method=False, local_factor=-1, global_factor=-1, damping=1e-12)’āü

A helper to set attributes. Sets all attributes which are related to the adaptive stepsize. Will overwrite all attributes and may thus cause some unwanted changes.

Parameters:

  • local_method (bool, str) ŌĆō has to be one of False, pade_10, pade_20, pade_11, pade_01, pade_02

  • global_method (bool, str) ŌĆō has to be one of False, pade_10, pade_20, pade_11, pade_01, pade_02

  • local_factor (float) ŌĆō sets the target error reduction

  • global_factor (float) ŌĆō sets the target error reduction

  • damping (float) ŌĆō a damping factor

Returns:

self

set_S_prime_params(local_method='projection', global_method='projection', gradient='intensity', newton=False, no_iterations=1)’āü

A helper to set attributes. Sets all attributes which are related to the updating of the signal field (signal_t -> signal_t_new). Will overwrite all attributes and may thus cause some unwanted changes.

(e.g. in the case of COPRA this may unintentionally overwrite the default of global_method=ŌĆØiterationŌĆØ)

Parameters:

  • local_method (str) ŌĆō has to be projection or iteration

  • global_method (str) ŌĆō has to be projection or iteration

  • gradient (str) ŌĆō has to be intensity or amplitude, defines which error function is used in iteration

  • newton (bool) ŌĆō if enabled a newton-style optimization is performed if method==iteration

  • weights (float, jnp.array) ŌĆō optional weights applied to the error function

  • no_iterations (int) ŌĆō the number of iterations if method==iteration

Returns:

self

create_initial_population(population_size=1, guess_type='random')’āü

Creates an initial population of pulses, parametrized as complex values on a grid.

Parameters:

  • population_size (int) ŌĆō the number of guesses to be optimized

  • guess_type (str) ŌĆō Has to be one of random, random_phase, constant or constant_phase.

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, jnp.array, jnp.array or None, jnp.array or None], initial populations for the pulse and possibly the gate-pulse in time domain or frequency domain for ChirpScans

shuffle_data_along_m(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Some solvers randomize local iterations. This is done through this method. It returns shuffled but consistent(!) data.

do_step_and_apply_momentum(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, do_step)’āü

If momentum is being used this wraps around the step-method of all solvers and updates the current guess accordingly.

momentum(population_size, eta)’āü

Needs to be called if momentum is meant to be used in the reconstruction.

Parameters:

  • population_size (int) ŌĆō is needed for some initialization

  • eta (float) ŌĆō parameter that controls the momentum strength

Returns:

the class instance

apply_momentum(signal, momentum, eta)’āü

Applies momentum to a signal.

Parameters:

  • signal ŌĆō jnp.array,

  • momentum ŌĆō Pytree, contains the velocity map and its update

  • eta (float) ŌĆō the strength of the momentum

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, Pytree], the updated signal and momentum state

calculate_error_population(population, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

What the names says. Returns the errors and the population.

post_process_create_trace(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, idx)’āü

Post processing to get the final trace

class core.base_classes_algorithms.GeneralOptimizationBASE(*args, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: AlgorithmsBASE

The Base-Class for all general solvers. Inherits from AlgorithmsBASE.

error_metric’āü

an arbitrary loss-function, needs to expect (trace, measured_trace) as input

Type:

Callable

create_initial_population(population_size, amp_type='gaussian', phase_type='polynomial', no_funcs_amp=5, no_funcs_phase=6)’āü

Creates an initial guess either explicit or parametrized. For bsplines one has to provide the B-Spline order k via bsplines_k

Parameters:

  • population_size (int) ŌĆō the number of individuals

  • amp_type (str) ŌĆō the representation of the spectral amplitude, can be one of gaussian, lorentzian, bsplines, continuous or an input for Classical Algorithms

  • phase_type (str) ŌĆō the representation of the spectral phase, can be one of polynomial, sinusoidal, sigmoidal, bsplines, continuous or an input for Classical Algorithms

  • no_funcs_amp (int) ŌĆō the number of basis functions for the spectral amplitude (only used if not on a grid)

  • no_funcs_phase (int) ŌĆō the number of basis functions for the spectral phase (only used if not on grid)

Returns:

Pytree, the initial guess population

split_population_in_amp_and_phase(population)’āü

Splits a population into an amplitude and phase population.

merge_population_from_amp_and_phase(population_amp, population_phase)’āü

Undoes split_population_in_amp_and_phase()

polynomial_term(coefficient, order, x0, x)’āü
polynomial_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info)’āü
sinusoidal_term(a, b, c, x)’āü
sinusoidal_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info)’āü
discrete_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info)’āü

tanh_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info)’āü
bspline_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info)’āü
gaussian_term(a, b, c, frequency)’āü
gaussian_amplitude(coefficients, measurement_info)’āü
lorentzian_term(a, b, c, frequency)’āü
lorentzian_amplitude(coefficients, measurement_info)’āü
discrete_amplitude(coefficients, measurement_info)’āü
bspline_amplitude(coefficients, measurement_info)’āü
get_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Evaluates the spectral phase onto the frequency axis.

get_amplitude(coefficients, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Evaluates the spectral amplitude onto the frequency axis.

make_pulse_f_from_individual(individual, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate='pulse')’āü

Evaluates a parametrized individual onto the frequency axis.

get_pulses_f_from_population(population, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Evaluates a parametrized population onto the frequency axis.

construct_trace(individual, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Generates a trace for a given individual. Calls the method specific function for calculating the nonlinear signal fields.

trace_error(trace, measured_trace)’āü

The least squares error.

calculate_error_individual(individual, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Calculates the error of an individual based on its trace. Allows modification of the error-function via error_metric() and loss_function_modification().

calculate_error_population(population, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Calls jax.vmap over calculate_error_individual() for an entire population.

initialize_general_optimizer(population)’āü

A common initialization step for all general solvers.

post_process_create_trace(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, idx)’āü

Post processing to get the final trace

core.base_classes_methods module’āü

class core.base_classes_methods.RetrievePulses(nonlinear_method, *args, cross_correlation=False, interferometric=False, key=None, seed=None, central_frequency=(None, None), **kwargs)’āü

Bases: object

The Base-Class for all reconstruction methods. Defines general initialization, preprocessing and postprocessing.

nonlinear_method’āü

SHG, THG, PG or SD

Type:

str

f0’āü

rarely some solvers need the central frequency to be zero. This saves the original central frequency.

Type:

float

doubleblind’āü

whether the reconstruction is supposed to yield the gate in addition to the pulse.

Type:

bool

spectrum_is_being_used’āü
Type:

bool

momentum_is_being_used’āü
Type:

bool

measurement_info’āü

a container of variable (but static) structure. Holds measurement data and parameters.

Type:

Pytree

descent_info’āü

a container of variable (but static) structure. Holds parameters of the reconstruction algorithm.

Type:

Pytree

descent_state’āü

a container of variable (but static) structure. Contains the current state of the solver.

Type:

Pytree

prng_seed’āü

seed for the key

Type:

int

key’āü

a jax.random.PRNGKey

Type:

jnp.array

factor’āü

for SHG/THG the a correction factor of 2/3 needs to applied occasionally.

Type:

int

theta’āü

an alias for the shifts/delays, internally indexed via m

Type:

jnp.array

time’āü

the time axis, internally indexed via k

Type:

jnp.array

frequency’āü

the frequency axis, internally indexed via n

Type:

jnp.array

measured_trace’āü

2D-array with the measured data. axis=0 corresponds to shift/delay (index m), axis=1 correpsonds to the frequencies (index n)

Type:

jnp.array

update_PRNG_key(seed)’āü
get_data(theta, frequency, measured_trace)’āü

Prepare/Convert data.

get_spectral_amplitude(measured_frequency, measured_spectrum, pulse_or_gate, **kwargs)’āü

Used to provide a measured pulse spectrum. A spectrum for the gate pulse can also be provided.

get_gate_pulse(frequency, gate_f)’āü

For crosscorrelation=True the actual gate pulse has to be provided.

get_calibration_curve(frequency, calibration_curve)’āü
plot_results(final_result, exact_pulse=None)’āü
get_individual_from_idx(idx, population)’āü
get_idx_best_individual(population)’āü

Calculates the error for a population. Returns the index of the fittest individual. And the population

get_idx_worst_individual(population)’āü

Calculates the error for a population. Returns the index of the worst individual. And the population

get_idx_average_individual(population)’āü

Calculates the error for a population. Returns the index of an average individual. And the population

post_process_get_pulse_and_gate(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, idx=None)’āü

FROG specific post processing to get the final pulse/gate

post_process_center_pulse_and_gate(pulse_t, gate_t, pulse_f, gate_f)’āü

This essentially removes the linear phase. But only approximately since no fits are done.

post_process(descent_state, error_arr)’āü

Creates the final_result object from the final descent_state.

class core.base_classes_methods.RetrievePulsesFROG(delay, frequency, measured_trace, nonlinear_method, cross_correlation=False, interferometric=False, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: RetrievePulses

The reconstruction class for FROG. Inherits from RetrievePulses.

  1. Trebino, ŌĆ£Frequency-Resolved Optical Gating: The Measurement of Ultrashort Laser PulsesŌĆØ, 10.1007/978-1-4615-1181-6 (2000)

tau_arr’āü

the delays

Type:

jnp.array

gate’āü

the gate-pulse (if its known).

jnp.array

an alias for tau_arr

jnp.array

idtheta’āü

an array with indices for tau_arr

Type:

jnp.array

dt’āü
Type:

float

df’āü
Type:

float

sk’āü

correction values for FFT->DFT

jnp.array

rn’āü

correction values for FFT->DFT

Type:

jnp.array

cross_correlation’āü
Type:

bool

interferometric’āü
Type:

bool

create_initial_population_doublepulse(population_size, **kwargs)’āü

Calls initial_guess_doublepulse.make_population_doublepulse to create an initial guess. The guess is in the time domain. Assumes an autocorrelation FROG.

Parameters:

  • population_size (int)

  • **kwargs ŌĆō passed to make_population_doublepulse()

Returns:

Pytree, the initial population

shift_signal_in_time(signal, tau, frequency, sk, rn)’āü

The Fourier-Shift theorem.

calculate_shifted_signal(signal, frequency, tau_arr, time, in_axes=(None, 0, None, None, None))’āü

The Fourier-Shift theorem applied to a list of signals.

calculate_signal_t(individual, tau_arr, measurement_info)’āü

Calculates the signal field of a FROG in the time domain.

Parameters:

  • individual (Pytree) ŌĆō a population containing only one member. (jax.vmap over whole population)

  • tau_arr (jnp.array) ŌĆō the delays

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains the measurement parameters (e.g. nonlinear method, interferometric, ŌĆ” )

Returns:

Pytree, contains the signal field in the time domain as well as the fields used to calculate it.

class core.base_classes_methods.RetrievePulses2DSI(delay, frequency, measured_trace, nonlinear_method, cross_correlation=False, spectral_filter1=None, spectral_filter2=None, tau_pulse_anc1=0, **kwargs)’āü

The reconstruction class for 2DSI.

1st filter in interferometer

jnp.array

delay between 1st interferometer arm and exterior pulse

float

anc_frequency1’āü

central frequency of pulse in 1st arm (calculated from max of spectral_filter1)

float

anc_frequency2’āü

central frequency of pulse in 2nd arm (calculated from max of spectral_filter2)

float

float

a 2D-array with phase values that could potentially have been applied to a pulse

jnp.array

class core.base_classes_methods.RetrievePulsesVAMPIRE(delay, frequency, measured_trace, nonlinear_method, cross_correlation=False, tau_interferometer=0, material_thickness=0, refractive_index=<refractiveindex.refractiveindex.RefractiveIndexMaterial object>, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: RetrievePulsesFROG

The reconstruction class for VAMPIRE.

[1] B. Seifert and H. Stolz, Meas. Sci. Technol. 20 (2009) 015303 (7pp), 10.1088/0957-0233/20/1/015303 (2008)

tau_interferometer’āü

delay of the interferometer arms

Type:

float

c0’āü

the speed of light

Type:

float

refractive_index’āü

returns the refractive index for a material given a wavelength in um

Type:

refractiveindex.RefractiveIndexMaterial, Callable

phase_matrix’āü

a 2D-array with phase values that could potentially have been applied to a pulse

Type:

jnp.array

get_phase_matrix(refractive_index, material_thickness, measurement_info)’āü

Calculates the phase matrix that is applied of a pulse passes through a material.

apply_phase(pulse_t, measurement_info, sk, rn)’āü

For an VAMPIRE reconstruction one may need to consider effects of material dispersion in the interferometer. This applies a dispersion based on phase_matrix in order to achieve this.

calculate_signal_t(individual, tau_arr, measurement_info)’āü

Calculates the signal field in the time domain.

Parameters:

  • individual (Pytree) ŌĆō a population containing only one member. (jax.vmap over whole population)

  • tau_arr (jnp.array) ŌĆō the delays

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains the measurement parameters (e.g. nonlinear method, ŌĆ” )

Returns:

Pytree, contains the signal field in the time domain as well as the fields used to calculate it.

class core.base_classes_methods.RetrievePulsesSTREAKING(delay_fs, energy_eV, measured_trace, Ip_eV=Array([[0]], dtype=int32), retrieve_dtme=False, cross_correlation='doubleblind', interferometric=False, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: RetrievePulsesFROG

The reconstruction class for Attosecond Streaking. Implements the single- and multi-channel Strong-Field-Approximation. All axis (time, frequency, ŌĆ” ) are converted to atomic units for convenience and because that standard with the Strong-Field-Approximation.

[1] R. Kienberger, E. Goulielmakis, M. Uiberacker, et al., Atomic transient recorder. Nature 427, 817-821 (2004), 10.1038/nature02277 [2] P. D. Keathley et al., (2016), New J. Phys. 18 073009, 10.1088/1367-2630/18/7/073009

energy_au’āü

the energy axis in atomic units

Type:

jnp.array

momentum_au’āü

the photoelectron momenta in atomic units

Type:

jnp.array

position_au’āü

the conjugate axis to momentum_au

Type:

jnp.array

sk_position_momentum’āü

same function as sk, but for the position-momentum fourier pair

Type:

jnp.array

rn_position_momentum’āü

same function as rn, but for the position-momentum fourier pair

Type:

jnp.array

Ip_au’āü

the ionization potential in atomic units, the number of channels is infered from the number of Ips

Type:

jnp.array

retrieve_dtme’āü

if true, the dipole-transition-matrix-elements will be retrieved

Type:

bool

dtme_momentum’āü

the optionally provided dipole-transition-matrix-elements

Type:

None, jnp.array

check_usability_of_time_axis(time, delay)’āü
convert_time_fs_au(time, unit_in, unit_out)’āü
convert_frequency_PHz_au(frequency, unit_in, unit_out)’āü
convert_energy_eV_au(energy, unit_in, unit_out)’āü
get_DTME(momentum_au, dtme_momentum)’āü
get_spectral_amplitude(measured_frequency_PHz, measured_spectrum, pulse_or_gate, cutoff_frequency_PHz=0.1, **kwargs)’āü

Used to provide a measured pulse spectrum. A spectrum for the gate pulse can also be provided. The cutoff_frequency_PHz is used if pulse_or_gate=ŌĆØpulseŌĆØ, for the vectorpotential the spectrum is divided by the frequency. In order to avoid dvergences the spectrum is set to zero below this cutoff.

make_volkov_phase_0(Ip, measurement_info)’āü
make_volkov_phase_1(pulse_t_nir_vectorpotential, measurement_info)’āü
make_dressed_DTME(dtme_position, pulse_t_nir_vectorpotential, measurement_info)’āü
make_streaking_amplitude(dtme_position, pulse_t_nir_vectorpotential, pulse_t_euv, measurement_info)’āü
calculate_signal_t(individual, tau_arr, measurement_info)’āü

Calculates the signal field in the time domain.

Parameters:

  • individual (Pytree) ŌĆō a population containing only one member. (jax.vmap over whole population)

  • tau_arr (jnp.array) ŌĆō the delays

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains the measurement parameters (e.g. nonlinear method, ŌĆ” )

Returns:

Pytree, contains the signal field in the time domain as well as the fields used to calculate it.

core.base_classic_algorithms module’āü

core.base_classic_algorithms.normalize_population(population, measurement_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_CG_state(shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_pseudo_newton_state(shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_lbfgs_state(shape, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.update_lbfgs_state(lbfgs_state, gamma, grad, descent_direction)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_linesearch_info(optimizer)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_S_prime_params(optimizer)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_newton_info(optimizer)’āü
core.base_classic_algorithms.initialize_descent_info(optimizer)’āü
class core.base_classic_algorithms.LSGPABASE(delay, frequency, measured_trace, nonlinear_method, cross_correlation=False, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: ClassicAlgorithmsBASE

The Least-Squares Generalized Projection Algorithm. Only available for delay based non-interferometric methods.

  1. Gagnon et al., Appl. Phys. B 92, 25-32, 10.1007/s00340-008-3063-x (2008)

update_pulse(signal_t_new, gate_shifted, measurement_info)’āü

Generates an new (maybe improoved) guess for the pulse.

update_gate(gate, signal_t, signal_t_new, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Generates an new (maybe improoved) guess for the gate. Uses an iterative formula. Thus not linear-least-squares anymore.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one iteration of the Vanilla Algorithm.

Parameters:

  • descent_state ŌĆō Pytree,

  • measurement_info ŌĆō Pytree,

  • descent_info ŌĆō Pytree,

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current errors

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population ŌĆō Pytree, the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state and the step-function of the algorithm.

class core.base_classic_algorithms.CPCGPABASE(delay, frequency, trace, nonlinear_method, cross_correlation=False, constraints=False, svd=False, antialias=False, shift_trace_to_zero=False, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: ClassicAlgorithmsBASE

The Constrained-PCGP-Algorithm. Only available for delay based non-interferometric methods. Transforms population to time domain during initialization.

    1. Kane and A. B. Vakhtin, Prog. Quantum Electron. 81 (100364), 10.1016/j.pquantelec.2021.100364 (2022)

constraints’āü

if true the operator based constraints are used.

Type:

bool

svd’āü

if true a full SVD is performed instead of a single iteration of the power method

Type:

bool

antialias’āü

if true anti-aliasing is applied to the outer-product-matrix-form

Type:

bool

get_spectral_amplitude(measured_frequency, measured_spectrum, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Used to provide a measured pulse spectrum. A spectrum for the gate pulse can also be provided.

calculate_opf(pulse_t, gate, pulse_t_prime, gate_prime, iteration, nonlinear_method, measurement_info)’āü

Calculates the opf given a pulse and gate.

calculate_signal_t_using_opf(individual, iteration, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Calculates signal_t for and individual via the opf.

do_anti_alias(opf, half_N)’āü

Performs anti-aliasing to the opf by setting a lower and upper an triangle to zero.

shift_rows(row, idx)’āü

Vectorized version of shift_rows. Takes similar arguments as shift_rows but with additional array axes over which shift_rows is mapped.

convert_opf_to_signal_t(opf, idx_arr)’āü

Transforms opf to signal field, by shifting along the time axis. Switching and flipping the two halfs around.

convert_signal_t_to_opf(signal_t, idx_arr)’āü

Converts a signal field into an opf by reversing the operations from convert_opf_to_signal_t().

decompose_opf(opf, pulse_t, gate, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Decomposes the opf into its dominant components via an SVD or the Power-Method.

impose_constraints(pulse_t, gate, opf, measurement_info)’āü

Applies additional constraints according to the operator formalism of PCGP.

update_individual(opf, individual, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Updates and individual using an updated opf.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one iteration of the C-PCGP Algorithm.

Parameters:

  • descent_state ŌĆō Pytree,

  • measurement_info ŌĆō Pytree,

  • descent_info ŌĆō Pytree,

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current errors

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population ŌĆō Pytree, the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state and the step-function of the algorithm.

post_process_create_trace(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, idx)’āü

For PCGP the trace is constructed using the opf.

do_step_and_apply_spectral_amplitude(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, do_step)’āü

If a spectrum is provided this wraps around the step-method of all solvers and projects the current guess onto the measured spectrum.

class core.base_classic_algorithms.GeneralizedProjectionBASE(*args, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: ClassicAlgorithmsBASE

Implements the Generalized Projection Algorithm.

    1. DeLong et al., Opt. Lett. 19, 2152-2154 (1994)

no_steps_descent’āü

the numer of descent steps per iteration

Type:

int

update_individual(individual, gamma, descent_direction, pulse_or_gate)’āü
update_population(population, gamma, descent_direction, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Applies the descent based update to the population.

get_Z_gradient(signal_t, signal_t_new, transform_arr, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the Z-error gradient for the entire population.

calc_Z_error_for_linesearch(gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the Z-error such that it can be called in a linesearch.

calc_Z_grad_for_linesearch(gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the Z-error gradient such that it can be called in a linesearch.

descent_Z_error_step(signal_t, signal_t_new, Z_error, descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Performs a descent step in order to minimize the Z-error. Employs gradient descent, nonlinear conjugate gradients, LBFGS or damped Newtons method (diagonal or full). The step size is determined via a fixed/adaptive step size, a backtracking or a zoom linesearch.

do_descent_Z_error_step(descent_state, signal_t_new, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Does one Z-error descent step. Calls descent_Z_error_step for pulse and or gate.

do_descent_Z_error(descent_state, signal_t_new, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs a descent based optimization to find the pulse/gate that are able to produce S_prime.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one iteration of the Generalized Projection Algorithm.

Parameters:

  • descent_state (Pytree)

  • measurement_info (Pytree)

  • descent_info (Pytree)

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current trace errors of the population.

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population (Pytree) ŌĆō the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state and the step-function of the algorithm.

class core.base_classic_algorithms.PtychographicIterativeEngineBASE(*args, pie_method='rPIE', **kwargs)’āü

Bases: ClassicAlgorithmsBASE

Implements a version of the Ptychographic Iterative Engine (PIE).

A. Maiden et al., Optica 4, 736-745 (2017) T. Schweizer, ŌĆ£Time-Domain Ptychography and its Applications in Ultrafast ScienceŌĆØ, PhD Thesis, Bern (2021)

alpha’āü

a regularization parameter

Type:

float

pie_method’āü

specifies the PIE variant. Can be one of None, PIE, ePIE, rPIE. Where None indicates that the pure gradient is used.

Type:

None, str

calculate_PIE_mu(signal_f_abs, trace_root, measurement_info, descent_info, local_or_global)’āü
calculate_PIE_trace(signal_f, measured_trace, measurement_info, descent_info, local_or_global)’āü
calculate_PIE_error(mu, signal_f, measured_trace)’āü

Calculates the normalized least-squares error using the amplitude residuals.

get_PIE_weights(probe, alpha, pie_method)’āü

Calculates the weight-functions for the differen PIE-version.

update_individual(individual, gamma, descent_direction, pulse_or_gate)’āü
update_population(population, gamma, descent_direction, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Applies the PIE update to the population.

calculate_PIE_descent_direction(signal_t, signal_t_new, transform_arr, pie_method, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the descent direction based on the PIE version.

calc_error_for_linesearch(gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the PIE-error such that it can be called in a linesearch.

calc_grad_for_linesearch(gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate, local_or_global)’āü

Calculates the PIE direction such that it can be called in a linesearch.

do_iteration(signal_t, signal_t_new, transform_arr, measured_trace, population, local_or_global_state, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate, local_or_global)’āü

Performs one local/global iteration of the PIE. On top of the different PIE-version nonlinear conjugate gradients, LBFGS or damped Newtons method (diagonal or full) may be used. The step size is determined via a fixed/adaptive step size, a backtracking or a zoom linesearch.

Newtons method with a full newton is not available for the reconstruction of the gate.

local_iteration(descent_state, transform_arr_m, trace_line, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Peforms one local iteration. Calls do_iteration() with the appropriate (randomized) signal fields.

local_step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one local iteration of the PIE. This means the method loops over the randomized measurement data once and updates the population using each data point individually.

Parameters:

  • descent_state (Pytree)

  • measurement_info (Pytree)

  • descent_info (Pytree)

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current trace errors of the population.

global_step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one global iteration of the PIE. This means the method updates the population once using all measured data at once.

Parameters:

  • descent_state (Pytree)

  • measurement_info (Pytree)

  • descent_info (Pytree)

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current trace errors of the population.

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population (Pytree) ŌĆō the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable, Callable], the initial descent state, the local and global step-functions of the algorithm.

run(population, no_iterations_local, no_iterations_global, **kwargs)’āü

The Algorithm can use a local and a global sequentially.

Parameters:

  • population (Pytree) ŌĆō the initial guess

  • no_iterations_local ŌĆō int, the number of local iterations. Accepts zero as a value.

  • no_iterations_global ŌĆō int, the number of globale iterations. Accepts zero as a value.

Returns:

Pytree, the final result

class core.base_classic_algorithms.COPRABASE(*args, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: ClassicAlgorithmsBASE

Implements a version of the Common Pulse Retrieval Algorithm (COPRA).

    1. Geib et al., Optica 6, 495-505 (2019)

update_individual(individual, gamma, descent_direction, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Updates an individual based on a descent direction and a step size.

update_population(population, gamma, descent_direction, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Applies the a descent based update to the population.

get_Z_gradient(signal_t, signal_t_new, transform_arr, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the Z-error gradient for the current population.

calc_Z_error_for_linesearch(gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the Z-error such that it can be called in a linesearch.

calc_Z_grad_for_linesearch(gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the Z-error gradient such that it can be called in a linesearch.

do_iteration(signal_t, signal_t_new, transform_arr, population, local_or_global_state, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate, local_or_global)’āü

Performs one local/global iteration of the Common Pulse Retrieval Algorithm. Uses gradient descent, nonlinear conjugate gradients, LBFGS or damped Newtons method (diagonal or full). The step size is determined via a fixed/adaptive step size, a backtracking or a zoom linesearch.

local_iteration(descent_state, transform_arr_m, trace_line, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Peforms one local iteration. Calls do_iteration() with the appropriate (randomized) signal fields.

local_step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one local iteration of the Common Pulse Retrieval Algorithm. This means the method loops over the randomized measurement data once and updates the population using each data point individually.

Parameters:

  • descent_state (Pytree)

  • measurement_info (Pytree)

  • descent_info (Pytree)

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current trace errors of the population.

global_step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one global iteration of the Common Pulse Retrieval Algorithm. This means the method updates the population once using all measured data at once.

Parameters:

  • descent_state (Pytree)

  • measurement_info (Pytree)

  • descent_info (Pytree)

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state and the current trace errors of the population.

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population (Pytree) ŌĆō the initial guess as created by create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable, Callable], the initial descent state, the local and global step-functions of the algorithm.

run(population, no_iterations_local, no_iterations_global, **kwargs)’āü

The Algorithm can use a local and a global sequentially.

Parameters:

  • population (Pytree) ŌĆō the initial guess

  • no_iterations_local ŌĆō int, the number of local iterations. Accepts zero as a value.

  • no_iterations_global ŌĆō int, the number of globale iterations. Accepts zero as a value.

Returns:

Pytree, the final result

class core.base_classic_algorithms.LSFBASE(*args, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: ClassicAlgorithmsBASE

Implements a version of the Linesearch FROG Algorithm (LSF). Despite its name the algorithm is NOT restricted to FROG.

    1. Krook and V. Pasiskevicius, Opt. Express 33, 33258-33269 (2025)

no_sections’āü

number of sections to split the search line into

Type:

int

number_of_disection_iterations’āü

as the name says

Type:

int

direction_mode’āü

can be random or continuous

Type:

str

ratio_points_for_continuous’āü

smaller value means more randomness/eratic

Type:

int

only_allow_improvements’āü

if true, only steps that decrease the error will be accepted

Type:

bool

apply_spectrum(pulse_f, spectral_amplitude, eta)’āü

Projects onto measured spectrum by dividing the current spectral amplitude out.

get_random_values(key, shape, minval, maxval, descent_info)’āü

LSF requires random directions. These are produced here.

get_search_direction_individual(keys, individual, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Creates a pytree with random search directions for one individual.

get_search_direction(key, population, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Creates a pytree with random search directions for an entire population.

get_scalars(direction, signal, descent_info)’āü

Calculates scalars to identify the min/max of a search direction.

select_new_brackets_N_section(E_arr, error_arr, descent_state, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Selects the two trial-individuals with the lowest errors in order to narrow the search bracket.

select_new_brackets_bisection(E_arr, error_arr, descent_state, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Narrows the search bracket by removing the trial-individual with the largest error or the one that is the furthest from the trial guess.

generate_E_arr(El, Er, n)’āü

Takes in a range El, Er and disects it into n equal sections.

bisection_step(El, Er, descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Does one bisection step of the LSF algorithm.

do_bisection_search(direction, descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Performs one bisection search to find the minimum along a given search direction. The number of iterations is set through self.number_of_bisection_iterations

search_along_direction(direction, descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs a bisection search along one direction for pulse and the for gate.

make_population_bisection_search(E_arr, population, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Combines the trial populations with the current population such that the errors may be computed.

calculate_error(E_arr, descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Calculates the trace error. Since pulse and gate are optimized independently the population as provided to calculate_error_individual() needs to be constructed from the current population and the fields in the cureent optimization.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one step of the LSF-algorithm.

Parameters:

  • descent_state ŌĆō Pytree,

  • measurement_info ŌĆō Pytree,

  • descent_info ŌĆō Pytree,

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state, errors of the current population

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population ŌĆō Pytree, the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state, the step-function of the algorithm.

core.base_general_optimization module’āü

core.base_general_optimization.loss_function_for_general(trace, measured_trace, exponent_trace=1, L_norm=1)’āü

An example of a modified loss function for general algorithms.

Inspired by ELFPIE: S. Zhang, T.T.J.M. Berendschot and J. Zhou, Signal Processing 210 109088, 10.1016/j.sigpro.2023.109088, (2023)

Parameters:

  • trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the current trace

  • measured_trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the measured trace

  • kwargs ŌĆō exponent_trace (int, float): the exponent applied to trace before building the residual L_norm (int, float): the norm exponent to be used

core.base_general_optimization.make_key_tree(key, pytree)’āü

For a given pytree, each leaf is replaced by a prng-key.

core.base_general_optimization.shuffle_pytree(key, pytree, axis)’āü

Randomizes the leafs of a given pytree along a given axis.

class core.base_general_optimization.DifferentialEvolutionBASE(*args, strategy='best1_bin', selection_mechanism='greedy', mutation_rate=0.5, crossover_rate=0.7, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: GeneralOptimizationBASE

Implements a Differential-Evolution Algorithm. Based on Qiang, J., Mitchell, C., A Unified Differential Evolution Algorithm for Global Optimization, 2014, https://www.osti.gov/servlets/purl/1163659

strategy’āü

the mutation and selection strategy, analogous to scipyŌĆÖs differential evolution.

Type:

str

mutation_rate’āü

the mutation rate

Type:

float

crossover_rate’āü

the crossover rate

Type:

float

selection_mechanism’āü

the selection mechanism, can be greedy or global, defined in select_population().

Type:

str

temperature’āü

a temperature value for the global selection mechanism

Type:

float

best1(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
best2(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
rand1(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
rand2(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
randtobest1(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
randtobest2(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
currenttorand1(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
currenttorand2(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
currenttobest1(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
currenttobest2(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü
make_bin_mask_tree(key, pytree, p)’āü

For a given pytree a random binary mask is generated on each leaf, via the probabilty p.

make_exp_mask_tree(key, pytree, p)’āü

For a given pytree a mask (e.g. of the form 111100000) is generated via the probability p on each row of each leaf.

bin_crossover(CR, parent_population, mutant_population, key)’āü

Peforms a binary crossover between two populations via the crossover rate CR.

exp_crossover(CR, parent_population, mutant_population, key)’āü

Peforms an exponential crossover between two populations via the crossover rate CR.

smooth_crossover(CR, parent_population, mutant_population, key)’āü

Similar to an exponential crossover but with a smooth nonbinary transition, which is fascilitated via a tanh function.

custom_mutation(F, best_individual, population, key)’āü

A placeholder to allow the introduction of custom mutation approaches.

custom_crossover(CR, parent_population, mutant_population, key)’āü

A placeholder to allow the introduction of custom crossover approaches.

do_mutation(mutation_strategy, F, best_individual, population, key)’āü

Creates and applies random mutations to a population.

do_crossover(crossover_strategy, CR, parent_population, mutant_population, key)’āü

Creates and applies random crossover events between two populations.

select_population(key, selection_mechanism, error_parent, error_trial, population_parent, population_trial, mu_parent, mu_trial, descent_info)’āü

Performs the evolutionary selection process for two populations. Currently selection_mechanism can be greedy or global.

greedy: implements a pairwise comparison between individuals of the two population, where always the individual with the higher fitness is selected. global: implements a comparison/ranking between all individuals of both populations. The next generation is selected via randomized sampling based on a Fermi-Distribution. A ŌĆ£temperatureŌĆØ allows tuning of this selection process.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one step/generation of the Differential Evolution Algorithm.

Parameters:

  • descent_state ŌĆō Pytree,

  • measurement_info ŌĆō Pytree,

  • descent_info ŌĆō Pytree,

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state, the mean, minimum and maximum error of the current population

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population ŌĆō Pytree, the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state, the step-function of the algorithm.

class core.base_general_optimization.EvosaxBASE(*args, solver=None, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: GeneralOptimizationBASE

Employs the evosax package to perform the optimization.

Robert Tjarko Lange, evosax: JAX-based Evolution Strategies, arXiv preprint arXiv:2212.04180 (2022)

solver’āü

any evosax-solver should work

Type:

evosax-solver

solver_params’āü

user defined parameters for the evosax-solver, if None the default params set in evosax are used

Type:

any

solver_kwargs’āü

some evosax-solver require additional input arguments. These can be supplied via this aattribute.

Type:

dict

step_amp_or_phase(population_amp_or_phase, descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info, amp_or_phase)’āü

Evosax does not respect the structure of a pytree. Thus when optimizing parameters with vastly different properties simultaneously one should expose them seperately to evosax in order to avoid mixing. Thus the step function is applied once to update the amplitudes and once to update the phases.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one optimization step through an evolutionary algorithm in evosax.

Parameters:

  • descent_state ŌĆō Pytree,

  • measurement_info ŌĆō Pytree,

  • descent_info ŌĆō Pytree,

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state, the mean, minimum and maximum error of the current population

initialize_evosax_solver(key, solver, population, individual, amp_or_phase)’āü

Initializes the provided evosax solver. The solvers need to be exposed to the entire population, one individual or the initial fitnesses. Since the amplitude and phase are optimized separetly a solver needs to be initialized once for each. (This done by calling this method twice.)

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Initializes the evosax-solver appropriately. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population ŌĆō Pytree, the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state, the step-function of the algorithm.

class core.base_general_optimization.AutoDiffBASE(*args, solver=None, **kwargs)’āü

Bases: GeneralOptimizationBASE

Employs the optimistix package to perform the optimization via Automatic-Differentiation.

J. Rader, T. Lyons and P.Kidger, Optimistix: modular optimisation in JAX and Equinox, arXiv:2402.09983 (2024) DeepMind et al., The DeepMind JAX Ecosystem, http://github.com/google-deepmind (2020)

solver’āü

solvers need to be initialized

Type:

optimistix-solver, optax-solver

alternating_optimization’āü

if true, the optimizer alternates between amplitude and phase

Type:

bool

optimize_group_delay’āü

if true, the group delay will be optimized instead of the spectral phase

Type:

bool

get_phase(coefficients, central_f, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Wraps around GeneralOptimizationBASE.get_phase() in order to fascilliate the optimization of the group delay.

loss_function(individual, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Wraps around self.calculate_error_individual() to return the error of the current guess.

loss_function_amp(individual_amp, individual_phase, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Helper to fascilliate optimization with respect to the amplitude only.

loss_function_phase(individual_phase, individual_amp, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Helper to fascilliate optimization with respect to the phase only.

step(descent_state, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Performs one optimization step.

Parameters:

  • descent_state ŌĆō Pytree,

  • measurement_info ŌĆō Pytree,

  • descent_info ŌĆō Pytree,

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, jnp.array], the updated descent state, errors of the current population

initialize_alternating_optimization(descent_state, population, solver, optimistix_args, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

If the amplitude and phase are supposed to be optimized in an alternating fashion instead of simultaneously, different methods have to be initialized.

initialize_optimistix(descent_state, solver, measurement_info, descent_info)’āü

Initializes the optimistix-solver.

initialize_run(population)’āü

Prepares all provided data and parameters for the reconstruction. Here the final shape/structure of descent_state, measurement_info and descent_info are determined.

Parameters:

population ŌĆō Pytree, the initial guess as created by self.create_initial_population()

Returns:

tuple[Pytree, Callable], the initial descent state, the step-function of the algorithm.

core.bsplines_1d module’āü

core.bsplines_1d.get_C_in(i, n)’āü

Naive implementation of n choose i.

core.bsplines_1d.get_m_ij(i, j, k)’āü

Calculates matrix elements (i,j) for a uniform bspline weight matrix of order k.

core.bsplines_1d.get_M(k)’āü

Calculates the weight matrix for uniform bsplines of order k.

core.bsplines_1d.get_prefactor(k)’āü

Calculates the prefactor for a weight matrix for uniform bsplines of order k.

core.bsplines_1d.make_bsplines(cpoints, k, M, f, Nx)’āü

Evaluate arbitrary order bsplines in 1D.

core.construct_s_prime module’āü

core.construct_s_prime.calculate_S_prime_projection(signal_t, measured_trace, mu, sk, rn)’āü

Calculates signal_t_new/S_prime via a projection onto the measured intensity.

Parameters:

  • signal_t (pytree) ŌĆō contains the complex signal field of the current guess

  • measured_trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the measured intensity

  • mu (float) ŌĆō the scaling factor between the measured intensity and the intensity of the current guess

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains measurement data and information

Returns:

jnp.array, the complex signal field in the time domain projected onto the measured intensity

core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_newton_diagonal_intensity(trace, measured_trace)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_newton_diagonal_amplitude(trace, measured_trace)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_newton_diagonal(signal_f, measured_trace, sk, rn, descent_info)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_gradient_intensity(mu, signal_f, measured_trace, sk, rn)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_gradient_amplitude(mu, signal_f, measured_trace, sk, rn)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_gradient(mu, signal_f, measured_trace, sk, rn, descent_info)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_descent_direction(signal_f, mu, measured_trace, sk, rn, descent_info)’āü

Calculates descent direction of the iterative calculation of signal_t_new/S_prime. Uses either gradient descent or newtons method with the diagonal approximation. The error-functions can be based on intensity or amplitude based residuals.

core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_error_intensity(mu, trace, measured_trace)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_error_amplitude(mu, trace, measured_trace)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_r_error(trace, measured_trace, mu, descent_info)’āü
core.construct_s_prime.calculate_S_prime_iterative_step(signal_t, measured_trace, mu, sk, rn, descent_info, local_or_global)’āü

One iteration of the iterative descent based calculation of signal_t_new/S_prime.

core.construct_s_prime.calculate_S_prime_iterative(signal_t, measured_trace, mu, sk, rn, descent_info, local_or_global)’āü

Calculates signal_t_new/S_prime via an iterative optimization of the least-squares error.

Parameters:

  • signal_t (jnp.array) ŌĆō the complex signal field in the time domain of the current guess

  • signal_t ŌĆō the complex signal field in the frequency domain of the current guess

  • measured_trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the measured intensity

  • mu (float) ŌĆō the scaling factor between the measured intensity and the intensity of the current guess

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains measurement data and information

  • descent_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains information on the behaviour of the solver

  • local_or_global (str) ŌĆō whether this is used in a local or global iteration

Returns:

jnp.array, the complex signal field in the time domain projected onto the measured intensity

core.construct_s_prime.calculate_S_prime(signal_t, measured_trace, mu, measurement_info, descent_info, local_or_global)’āü

Calculates signal_t_new/S_prime via projection or iterative optimization

Parameters:

  • signal_t (jnp.array) ŌĆō the complex signal field in the time domain of the current guess

  • signal_f (jnp.array) ŌĆō the complex signal field in the frequency domain of the current guess

  • measured_trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the measured intensity

  • mu (float) ŌĆō the scaling factor between the measured intensity and the intensity of the current guess

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains measurement data and information

  • descent_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains information on the behaviour of the solver

  • local_or_global (str) ŌĆō whether this is used in a local or global iteration

Returns:

jnp.array, the complex signal field in the time domain projected onto the measured intensity

core.create_population module’āü

core.create_population.get_initial_amp(measurement_info)’āü

Estimate spectral amplitude from the integrated measured intensity. For SHG/THG the amplitude is interpolated to lie in the correct frequency region.

core.create_population.random(key, shape)’āü
core.create_population.random_phase(key, shape, amp)’āü
core.create_population.constant(key, shape)’āü
core.create_population.constant_phase(key, shape, amp)’āü
core.create_population.create_population_classic(key, population_size, guess_type, measurement_info)’āü

Creates a stack of initial guesses with the shape (population_size, jnp.size(frequency)). The guesses are all in the frequency domain. The created populations can be optimized by both general and classical solvers.

Parameters:

  • key (jnp.array) ŌĆō jax.random.PRNGKey

  • population_size (int) ŌĆō the number of guesses to be optimized

  • guess_type (str) ŌĆō the guess mode. Has to be one of random, random_phase, constant or constant_phase. doublepulse is moved to initial_guess_doublepulse.py

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds the measurement information, is filled during initialization of each solver

Returns:

jnp.array, stack of 1D-arrays

core.create_population.polynomial_guess(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.sinusoidal_guess(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.sigmoidal_guess(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.bspline_guess_phase(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.continuous_discrete_guess_phase(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.gaussian_or_lorentzian_guess(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.continuous_discrete_guess_amp(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.bspline_guess_amp(key, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.general_phase(key, population, shape, measurement_info, phase_type)’āü
core.create_population.general_amp(key, population, shape, measurement_info, amp_type)’āü
core.create_population.create_phase(key, phase_type, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.create_amp(key, amp_type, population, shape, measurement_info)’āü
core.create_population.create_population_general(key, amp_type, phase_type, population, population_size, no_funcs_amp, no_funcs_phase, spectrum_provided, measurement_info, pulse_or_gate)’āü

Creates an initial guess population for general solvers. Since general solvers do not require a grid, different representations for amplitude and phase can be used. The population is represented in the freqeuncy domain.

Parameters:

  • key (jnp.array) ŌĆō jax.random.PRNGKey

  • amp_type (str) ŌĆō representation of the amplitude

  • phase_type (str) ŌĆō representation of the phase

  • population (Pytree) ŌĆō a MyNamespace object containing amp and phase

  • population_size (int) ŌĆō the number of individuals to optimize

  • no_funcs_amp (int) ŌĆō some representations can consist of multiple basis functions

  • no_funcs_phase (int) ŌĆō some representations can consist of multiple basis functions

  • spectrum_provided (bool) ŌĆō if a spectrum is provided then the guessed population will not include an amplitude

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds the measurement information, is filled during initialization of each solver

  • pulse_or_gate (str) ŌĆō only needed in streaking, there the scale of the nir/ir-vectorpotential needs to be always optimizable

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, Pytree]

core.initial_guess_doublepulse module’āü

core.initial_guess_doublepulse.gaussian(x, a, b, c)’āü

Gaussian for fit.

core.initial_guess_doublepulse.get_double_pulse_amps(trace, sigma=10, init_std=0.001)’āü

Estimate temporal amplitude based on Trace. Takes average along frequency axis. Fits gaussians and constructs guess from fit result.

core.initial_guess_doublepulse.get_flat_spectral_phase(time, freq, trace, nonlinear_method, monochromatic=True, sigma=10)’āü

Construct linear spectral phase in time domain.

core.initial_guess_doublepulse.get_double_pulse_initial_guess(tau_arr, frequency, measured_trace, nonlinear_method, monochromatic_double_pulse=True, sigma=3, init_std=0.001, i_want_control=False)’āü

Finds an initial guess for an autocorrelation FROG trace for doublepulses. Works by finding the delays, amplitudes and central frequencies through scipyŌĆÖs peak finder and gaussian fits. Does use or work with jax.

Parameters:

  • tau_arr (jnp.array) ŌĆō the delay axis 1D-array

  • frequency (jnp.array) ŌĆō the frequency axis 1D-array

  • measured_trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the measured trace 2D-array

  • nonlinear_method (str) ŌĆō the nonlinear method

  • monochromatic_double_pulse (bool) ŌĆō whether the doublepulses had different central frequencies

  • sigma (float) ŌĆō a smoothing parameter

  • init_std (float) ŌĆō initial value for width of gaussian fits

  • i_want_control (bool) ŌĆō this method does not really work and is disabled for SHG/THG, can be overwritten by this

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, jnp.array], guess in time and frequency domain

core.initial_guess_doublepulse.make_population_doublepulse(key, population_size, tau_arr, frequency, measured_trace, nonlinear_method, monochromatic_double_pulse=True, sigma=3, init_std=0.001, i_want_control=False)’āü

Create an initial guess population for a doublepulse using get_double_pulse_initial_guess, with its restrictions. The population is constructed from a deterministic doublepulse guess by adding noise.

Parameters:

  • key (jnp.array) ŌĆō a jax.random.PRNGKey

  • population_size (int) ŌĆō the number of initial guesses

  • tau_arr (jnp.array) ŌĆō the delay axis 1D-array

  • frequency (jnp.array) ŌĆō the frequency axis 1D-array

  • measured_trace (jnp.array) ŌĆō the measured trace 2D-array

  • nonlinear_method (str) ŌĆō the nonlinear method

  • monochromatic_double_pulse (bool) ŌĆō whether the doublepulses had different central frequencies

  • sigma (float) ŌĆō a smoothing parameter

  • init_std (float) ŌĆō initial value for width of gaussian fits

  • i_want_control (bool) ŌĆō this method does not really work and is disabled for SHG/THG, can be overwritten by this

Returns:

jnp.array

core.lbfgs module’āü

core.lbfgs.backward_loop(q, i, rho, s, y)’āü

Constructing the LBFGS direction without materializing the inverse hessian is done through nested vector-multiplications.

core.lbfgs.forward_loop(r, i, alpha, rho, s, y)’āü

Constructing the LBFGS direction without materializing the inverse hessian is done through nested vector-multiplications.

core.lbfgs.calculate_quasi_newton_direction(grad_current, grad_prev, rho, s, y, newton_info)’āü

Does the actual LBFGS calculation.

core.lbfgs.do_lbfgs(grad_current, lbfgs_state, descent_info)’āü

Prepares and calls the LBFGS calculation.

core.lbfgs.get_quasi_newton_direction(grad, lbfgs_state, descent_info)’āü

Calculate the quasi-newton direciton using LBFGS.

Parameters:

  • grad (jnp.array) ŌĆō the current gradient

  • lbfgs_stat (Pytree) ŌĆō the current lbfgs state

  • descent_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds information on the solver (e.g. memory size for LBFGS)

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, Pytree], the quasi-newton direction and the unchanged lbfgs_state

core.nonlinear_cg module’āü

core.nonlinear_cg.get_nonlinear_CG_direction(descent_direction, cg, beta_parameter_version)’āü

Calculates the descent direction using Nonlinear Conjugate Gradients.

Parameters:

  • descent_direction (jnp.array) ŌĆō the descent direction from a descent solver

  • cg (Pytree) ŌĆō the current conjugate gradient state, holds the previous descent direction etc.

  • beta_parameter_version (str) ŌĆō the concrete NCG method to use

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, Pytree], the descent direction based on NCG, the updated NCG state

core.nonlinear_cg.get_beta(grad, grad_prev, descent_direction_prev, beta_parameter_version)’āü

Calls the NCG-parameter functions.

core.nonlinear_cg.beta_fletcher_reeves(grad, grad_prev, descent_direction_prev)’āü

Compute beta based on Fletcher-Reeves.

core.nonlinear_cg.beta_polak_ribiere(grad, grad_prev, descent_direction_prev)’āü

Compute beta based one Polak-Ribiere.

core.nonlinear_cg.beta_hestenes_stiefel(grad, grad_prev, descent_direction_prev)’āü

Compute beta based on Hestenes-Stiefel.

core.nonlinear_cg.beta_dai_yuan(grad, grad_prev, descent_direction_prev)’āü

Compute beta based on Dai-Yuan.

core.nonlinear_cg.beta_average(grad, grad_prev, descent_direction_prev)’āü

Compute beta as an average of the existing methods.

core.phase_matrix_funcs module’āü

core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_group_delay_phase(refractive_index, n_arr, k0_arr, wavelength_0, wavelength)’āü

Uses finite differences to get the group index at a specfific wavelength and returns the linearized group delay per mm at that wavelength.

core.phase_matrix_funcs.calculate_phase_matrix_material(measurement_info, parameters, central_f)’āü

Calculates a phase matrix via material dispersion. Not differentiable due to usage of refractiveindex.

Parameters:

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds measurement data and parameters, needs to contain the material thickness theta in mm.

  • parameters (refractiveindex.RefractiveIndexMaterial) ŌĆō an object providing the refractive index, the speed of light in m/s

Returns:

jnp.array, the calculated phase matrix

core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_sine_phase(omega, phase_shift, parameters)’āü
core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_tanh_phase(omega, phase_shift, parameters)’āü
core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_gaussian_phase(omega, phase_shift, parameters)’āü
core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_MIIPS_phase(omega, phase_shift, parameters)’āü
core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_G_MIIPS_phase(omega, phase_shift, parameters)’āü
core.phase_matrix_funcs.calculate_phase_matrix(measurement_info, parameters, phase_func=<function calc_MIIPS_phase>)’āü

Calculates a phase matrix using a specified phase function.

Parameters:

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds measurement data and parameters, needs to contain the shift-values in appropriate units

  • parameters (tuple) ŌĆō the parameters which are expected by phase_func

  • phase_func (Callable) ŌĆō defines how the phase is calculated

Returns:

jnp.array, the calculated phase matrix

core.phase_matrix_funcs.calc_GDD(omega, phase_shift, parameters, phase_func=<function calc_sine_phase>)’āü

Uses jax.grad to get the GDD of phase_func. Thus doesnt work with non-differentiable functions. Assumes phase_func is holomorphic.

core.spectral_filter_funcs module’āü

core.spectral_filter_funcs.gaussian_filter(frequency, parameters, filter_dict)’āü
core.spectral_filter_funcs.lorentzian_filter(frequency, parameters, filter_dict)’āü
core.spectral_filter_funcs.rectangular_filter(frequency, parameters, filter_dict)’āü
core.spectral_filter_funcs.multi_filter(frequency, parameters, filter_dict)’āü
core.spectral_filter_funcs.get_filter(filter_type, frequency, parameters, custom_func=None, refractive_index=<refractiveindex.refractiveindex.RefractiveIndexMaterial object>, material_thickness=0)’āü

Generate a spectral filter.

Parameters:

  • filter_func (str, tuple[str]) ŌĆō can be one of `gaussian, lorentzian, rectangular, multi or custom`. For multi, parameters needs to specify the repsective filetr function

  • frequency (jnp.array) ŌĆō the frequency axis

  • parameters (tuple) ŌĆō the parameters required by the filter function, have the input form (a, f0, fwhm, p)

  • custom_func (Callable, None) ŌĆō in case of filter_func=ŌĆØcustomŌĆØ the custom filter function needs to be provided here.

  • refractive_index (refractiveindex.RefractiveIndexMaterial) ŌĆō provides the refractive index

  • material_thickness (float, int) ŌĆō the material thickness in millimeters

Returns:

jnp.array, the spectral filter on the frequency axis

core.spectral_filter_funcs.get_phase_matrix(refractive_index, material_thickness, frequency, central_frequency)’āü

Calculates the phase matrix that is applied of a pulse passes through a material.

core.stepsize module’āü

core.stepsize.backtracking_linesearch(linesearch_state, error_func, grad_func, linesearch_params, linesearch_info, measurement_info)’āü

Perform one iteration of an Armijo-style linesearch.

Parameters:

  • linesearch_state (Pytree) ŌĆō contains the armijo condition, gamma and iteration

  • error_func (Callable) ŌĆō calculates the error to be optimized, expects gamma, linesearch_info and measurement_info

  • grad_func (Callable) ŌĆō unused

  • linesearch_params (Pytree) ŌĆō contains parameters for the linesearch iteration

  • linesearch_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains variables related to the initial state of the linesearch

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains measurement data and parameters

Returns:

MyNamespace, the updated linesearch_state

core.stepsize.finding_phase(current_vals, bracket, linesearch_info, linesearch_params)’āü
core.stepsize.zoom_interpolation(low_vals, high_vals)’āü
core.stepsize.zoom_phase(current_vals, bracket, linesearch_info, linesearch_params)’āü
core.stepsize.zoom_linesearch(linesearch_state, error_func, grad_func, linesearch_params, linesearch_info, measurement_info)’āü

Perform one iteration of an zoom linesearch. Works in two phases. In the first phase an interval containing a minimum is located by increasing the step size and observation of the behavior of the error and error-gradient. If a suitable interval has been located the minimum is approximated/found through successive cubic interpolation. The search terminates if the Armijo- and Wolfe conditions are met or one runs out of iterations.

Parameters:

  • linesearch_state (Pytree) ŌĆō contains information on the current state of the linesearch

  • error_func (Callable) ŌĆō calculates the error to be optimized, expects gamma, linesearch_info and measurement_info

  • grad_func (Callable) ŌĆō calculates the error-gradient to be optimized, expects gamma, linesearch_info and measurement_info

  • linesearch_params (Pytree) ŌĆō contains parameters for the linesearch iteration

  • linesearch_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains variables related to the initial state of the linesearch

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō contains measurement data and parameters

Returns:

MyNamespace, the updated linesearch_state

core.stepsize.do_linesearch_step(linesearch_state, linesearch_info, measurement_info, linesearch_params, error_func, grad_func)’āü
core.stepsize.end_linesearch(linesearch_state, max_steps_linesearch)’āü
core.stepsize.do_linesearch(linesearch_info, measurement_info, descent_info, error_func, grad_func, local_or_global)’āü

Perform a linesearch to obtain an improved step size in a descent based optimization.

Parameters:

  • linesearch_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds information on the initial state at gamma=0

  • measurement_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds measurement data and parameters

  • descent_info (Pytree) ŌĆō holds parameters of the descent algorithm

  • error_func (Callable) ŌĆō calculates the error to be optimized, expects gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info

  • grad_func (Callable) ŌĆō calculates the gradient of error_func, expects gamma, linesearch_info, measurement_info

  • local_or_global (str) ŌĆō whether this is used in a local or global iteration

Returns:

float, the approximated optimal step size

core.stepsize.get_scaling(gradient, descent_direction, xi, local_or_global_state, pulse_or_gate, local_or_global)’āü
core.stepsize.adaptive_step_size(error, gradient, descent_direction, xi, local_or_global_state, adaptive_scaling_info, pulse_or_gate, local_or_global)’āü

Calculate an improved step size based through a pade approximation of the error function at the current position.

Parameters:

  • error (float) ŌĆō the current error

  • gradient (jnp.array) ŌĆō the current gradient

  • descent_direction (jnp.array) ŌĆō the current descent direction

  • local_or_global_state (Pytree) ŌĆō holds information of the current descent_state

  • xi (float) ŌĆō a damping factor to avoid division by zero

  • order (str) ŌĆō the pade-approximation to be used, can be one of pade_10 (linear), pade_20 (nonlinear), pade_01, pade_11 or pade_02

  • pulse_or_gate (str) ŌĆō whether this is applied to pulse or gate

  • local_or_global (str) ŌĆō whether this happens inside a local or global iteration

Returns:

tuple[jnp.array, Pytree], the scaled descent direction and the local_or_global_state
